text.title: Image Generation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for image-gen pipeline config.

rule.statement: Pick model by task class: text-in-image → DALL-E 3 / GPT-4o; photorealism → Flux Pro; brand identity → SD + LoRA or Flux Kontext; bulk volume → Flux schnell (Apache 2.0, self-hosted).
rule.rationale: Per-task strength profiles dominate quality and cost. Wrong model = either bad output or 5-10x cost.

rule.statement: Every prompt has six dimensions: Subject + Style + Lighting + Composition + Details + Technical. Missing any one regresses to noisy / flat output.

rationale.source: DALL-E 3 prompt guide; Flux prompt docs

rule.rationale: Each dimension controls a separate visual axis; omissions yield inconsistent regenerations and burn retry budget.

rule.statement: For batch ≥ 1000 images/day, self-host Flux schnell. Do NOT pay-per-call on DALL-E for bulk pipelines.
Brand-consistent series requires LoRA fine-tune (10-20 reference images) OR Flux Kontext with character anchor. Prompt-keyword "in brand style" alone is unreliable.

rationale.source: LoRA training research; brand consistency audits

rule.rationale: Standard API models produce visually different outputs on identical prompts; only fine-tune or reference workflow preserves identity.

rule.statement: Pipeline rejects prompts mentioning real named people for photorealism. Returns canonical refusal + alerts on attempt.

rationale.source: OpenAI usage policy 2026; EU AI Act art. 50

rule.rationale: Likeness rights + content policy violation; both legal and platform risk.
